name: ExecuteAllScenarios determinism across 20 runs
aim: ReplayEngine.ExecuteAllScenarios() must produce identical FinalFragility for all 6 scenarios across 20 runs

threshold.rationale: ComputeTopologySensitivity is a pure function of GraphSnapshot — any deviation indicates non-determinism

on_exceed: Non-deterministic sensitivity computation means replay golden files cannot be trusted — CI will produce random pass/fail results for the same commit

answered_questions.what_was_tested: ExecuteAllScenarios() called 20 times; FinalFragility and PressureHeatmap checksum compared per scenario per run
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 1e-9 is float64 arithmetic noise floor — any larger deviation is a real non-determinism bug, not precision
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Golden file written from run 0 will mismatch run 1 — regression tests produce false alarms every CI build
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: SHA256 of (fragility, pressure_heatmap) per scenario compared across all runs
answered_questions.is_golden_file_frozen: Written on first run if absent; never overwritten automatically

name: ComputeTopologySensitivity stability across 100 identical calls
aim: ComputeTopologySensitivity called 100 times on identical GraphSnapshot must produce zero deviations
golden_file: N/A — comparison is internal across runs

threshold.rationale: ComputeTopologySensitivity takes no time/random inputs — it must be a pure function

on_exceed: ComputeTopologySensitivity has hidden state (global variable, map iteration order, time.Now()) → dashboard shows different fragility scores on each orchestrator tick for the same topology

answered_questions.what_was_tested: ComputeTopologySensitivity called 100 times on same GraphSnapshot (keystone collapse: 7 nodes, 6 edges)
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 1e-12 is below float64 arithmetic rounding — any deviation is a state side-effect, not precision
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Orchestrator tick produces different fragility values on consecutive calls → dashboard flickers → operator can't trust readings
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: Direct float64 comparison with 1e-12 epsilon across 100 calls

name: InjectTopologySnapshot capture fidelity
aim: ReplayCapture.FinalFragility must equal ComputeTopologySensitivity output; PressureHeatmap must contain all nodes with valid values; EdgeWeightChanges must cover all edges

threshold.rationale: InjectTopologySnapshot must faithfully report ComputeTopologySensitivity output

on_exceed: Layer 4 regression tests compare against ReplayCapture values — if those values do not faithfully reflect the topology, golden files are built on wrong reference data

answered_questions.what_was_tested: InjectTopologySnapshot on 4 scenarios; capture fields compared to direct ComputeTopologySensitivity output
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 1e-9 — the values should be identical since InjectTopologySnapshot calls ComputeTopologySensitivity internally
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Golden files written from wrong values — regression detection broken from the start
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: Direct comparison of capture.FinalFragility against ComputeTopologySensitivity(same snap)

name: Topology sensitivity golden file comparison — 6 scenarios
aim: ComputeTopologySensitivity output for all 6 scenarios must match golden files within 0.0010%

threshold.rationale: Any change in fragility/perturbation scores changes what operators see in the dashboard risk panel

on_exceed: A code change in topology_sensitivity.go silently altered the SystemFragility or PerturbationScore values that the dashboard renders — operators see wrong risk information

answered_questions.what_was_tested: ComputeTopologySensitivity on 6 topology snapshots: keystone_collapse, diamond_stress, linear_cascade, recovery_step_1, recovery_step_2, recovery_step_3
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 0.001% tolerance covers float64 arithmetic variance; larger change is a real behavioral difference that changes dashboard values
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard fragility scores and service perturbation rankings differ from expected — operators may prioritise wrong services
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: Golden written on first run from actual runtime output; compared on every subsequent run
answered_questions.is_golden_file_frozen: Yes — update only when the topology sensitivity algorithm is intentionally changed

name: Builder ExpectedFragility values match ComputeTopologySensitivity output
aim: Each scenario builder's ExpectedFragility must be within 5.0% of actual ComputeTopologySensitivity output
golden_file: N/A — no golden needed, threshold is from builder declarations

threshold.rationale: 5% tolerance — builder expected values are approximate documentation; exact match not required but large deviation means stale docs

on_exceed: Scenario builders declare fragility values that no longer match computation — documentation and test expectations diverged from reality

answered_questions.what_was_tested: ExpectedFragility in each of the 6 builder functions vs actual ComputeTopologySensitivity output
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 5% — these are declared approximations; exact match is not the contract, but >5% means the algorithm changed significantly
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Test documentation claims wrong fragility values — operators briefed on wrong expected behaviour
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: Single run — cross-run determinism verified in L4-REP-001
answered_questions.how_to_update_golden_file: Update ExpectedFragility in each builder function in internal/integration/replay.go

name: Version regression: all scenarios within 0.01% of golden
aim: Current build must produce topology sensitivity within 0.0100% of stored golden for all 6 scenarios

threshold.rationale: Any change in sensitivity algorithm must be intentional and explicitly reviewed — 0.01% catches real changes, not float noise

on_exceed: Topology sensitivity algorithm changed between this commit and the golden-writing commit — review internal/modelling/topology_sensitivity.go diff before proceeding

answered_questions.what_was_tested: ComputeTopologySensitivity output for 6 scenarios compared against golden files written by L4-REP-002
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 0.01% is tighter than the write tolerance (0.001%) to catch regression; still above float64 noise floor
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: An algorithm change altered risk scores — operators may see wrong fragility or perturbation rankings
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: Same inputs as L4-REP-002; golden files are the invariant anchor
answered_questions.is_golden_file_frozen: Yes — update ONLY when algorithm change is intentional and reviewed
answered_questions.how_to_update_golden_file: Delete golden files and re-run L4-REP-002, then confirm all scenarios produce expected outputs

name: Triangle recovery convergence within 1% of baseline
aim: After 3 recovery steps, fragility must be within 1.0% of healthy baseline

threshold.rationale: Step 3 edges are nearly identical to healthy baseline — convergence within 1% validates the recovery model

on_exceed: Recovery scenario step 3 does not return to baseline within 1% — either BuildTriangleRecoveryScenario(3) edges need updating or sensitivity is over-penalising residual degradation

answered_questions.what_was_tested: Triangle recovery fragility across 3 steps; step 3 convergence to healthy baseline
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 1% matches the topology_replay_dashboard_test.go acceptance criterion of 0.98-1.00 fragility
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard shows incomplete recovery even after all edges are restored — operators confused about system health
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: All topology inputs are fixed (no time/random) — computation is deterministic

name: ExecuteAllScenarios end-to-end regression vs golden
aim: Full replay pipeline output must match golden within 0.0100% per scenario

threshold.rationale: End-to-end regression via full replay pipeline — catches wrapping/modification in InjectTopologySnapshot

on_exceed: Full replay pipeline produces different fragility than direct ComputeTopologySensitivity — InjectTopologySnapshot is transforming or overwriting the computed sensitivity

answered_questions.what_was_tested: ExecuteAllScenarios() output compared to golden written by L4-REP-001
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 0.01% — pipeline should be identical to isolated function; any delta indicates unwanted transformation
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Replay pipeline does not faithfully reflect topology sensitivity — golden comparisons are unreliable
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: L4-REP-001 already verified determinism; this test only checks regression

answered_questions.what_was_tested: Triangle recovery fragility across 3 steps; step 3 convergence to healthy baseline
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 1% matches the topology_replay_dashboard_test.go acceptance criterion of 0.98-1.00 fragility
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard shows incomplete recovery even after all edges are restored — operators confused about system health
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: All topology inputs are fixed (no time/random) — computation is deterministic

name: ExecuteAllScenarios end-to-end regression vs golden
aim: Full replay pipeline output must match golden within 0.0100% per scenario

threshold.rationale: End-to-end regression via full replay pipeline — catches wrapping/modification in InjectTopologySnapshot

on_exceed: Full replay pipeline produces different fragility than direct ComputeTopologySensitivity — InjectTopologySnapshot is transforming or overwriting the computed sensitivity

answered_questions.what_was_tested: ExecuteAllScenarios() output compared to golden written by L4-REP-001
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 0.01% — pipeline should be identical to isolated function; any delta indicates unwanted transformation
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Replay pipeline does not faithfully reflect topology sensitivity — golden comparisons are unreliable
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: L4-REP-001 already verified determinism; this test only checks regression

name: ExecuteAllScenarios determinism across 20 runs
aim: ReplayEngine.ExecuteAllScenarios() must produce identical FinalFragility for all 6 scenarios across 20 runs

threshold.rationale: ComputeTopologySensitivity is a pure function of GraphSnapshot — any deviation indicates non-determinism

on_exceed: Non-deterministic sensitivity computation means replay golden files cannot be trusted — CI will produce random pass/fail results for the same commit

answered_questions.what_was_tested: ExecuteAllScenarios() called 20 times; FinalFragility and PressureHeatmap checksum compared per scenario per run
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 1e-9 is float64 arithmetic noise floor — any larger deviation is a real non-determinism bug, not precision
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Golden file written from run 0 will mismatch run 1 — regression tests produce false alarms every CI build
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: SHA256 of (fragility, pressure_heatmap) per scenario compared across all runs
answered_questions.is_golden_file_frozen: Written on first run if absent; never overwritten automatically

name: ComputeTopologySensitivity stability across 100 identical calls
aim: ComputeTopologySensitivity called 100 times on identical GraphSnapshot must produce zero deviations
golden_file: N/A — comparison is internal across runs

threshold.rationale: ComputeTopologySensitivity takes no time/random inputs — it must be a pure function

on_exceed: ComputeTopologySensitivity has hidden state (global variable, map iteration order, time.Now()) → dashboard shows different fragility scores on each orchestrator tick for the same topology

answered_questions.what_was_tested: ComputeTopologySensitivity called 100 times on same GraphSnapshot (keystone collapse: 7 nodes, 6 edges)
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 1e-12 is below float64 arithmetic rounding — any deviation is a state side-effect, not precision
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Orchestrator tick produces different fragility values on consecutive calls → dashboard flickers → operator can't trust readings
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: Direct float64 comparison with 1e-12 epsilon across 100 calls

name: InjectTopologySnapshot capture fidelity
aim: ReplayCapture.FinalFragility must equal ComputeTopologySensitivity output; PressureHeatmap must contain all nodes with valid values; EdgeWeightChanges must cover all edges

threshold.rationale: InjectTopologySnapshot must faithfully report ComputeTopologySensitivity output

on_exceed: Layer 4 regression tests compare against ReplayCapture values — if those values do not faithfully reflect the topology, golden files are built on wrong reference data

answered_questions.what_was_tested: InjectTopologySnapshot on 4 scenarios; capture fields compared to direct ComputeTopologySensitivity output
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 1e-9 — the values should be identical since InjectTopologySnapshot calls ComputeTopologySensitivity internally
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Golden files written from wrong values — regression detection broken from the start
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: Direct comparison of capture.FinalFragility against ComputeTopologySensitivity(same snap)

name: Topology sensitivity golden file comparison — 6 scenarios
aim: ComputeTopologySensitivity output for all 6 scenarios must match golden files within 0.0010%

threshold.rationale: Any change in fragility/perturbation scores changes what operators see in the dashboard risk panel

on_exceed: A code change in topology_sensitivity.go silently altered the SystemFragility or PerturbationScore values that the dashboard renders — operators see wrong risk information

answered_questions.what_was_tested: ComputeTopologySensitivity on 6 topology snapshots: keystone_collapse, diamond_stress, linear_cascade, recovery_step_1, recovery_step_2, recovery_step_3
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 0.001% tolerance covers float64 arithmetic variance; larger change is a real behavioral difference that changes dashboard values
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard fragility scores and service perturbation rankings differ from expected — operators may prioritise wrong services
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: Golden written on first run from actual runtime output; compared on every subsequent run
answered_questions.is_golden_file_frozen: Yes — update only when the topology sensitivity algorithm is intentionally changed

name: Builder ExpectedFragility values match ComputeTopologySensitivity output
aim: Each scenario builder's ExpectedFragility must be within 5.0% of actual ComputeTopologySensitivity output
golden_file: N/A — no golden needed, threshold is from builder declarations

threshold.rationale: 5% tolerance — builder expected values are approximate documentation; exact match not required but large deviation means stale docs

on_exceed: Scenario builders declare fragility values that no longer match computation — documentation and test expectations diverged from reality

answered_questions.what_was_tested: ExpectedFragility in each of the 6 builder functions vs actual ComputeTopologySensitivity output
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 5% — these are declared approximations; exact match is not the contract, but >5% means the algorithm changed significantly
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Test documentation claims wrong fragility values — operators briefed on wrong expected behaviour
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: Single run — cross-run determinism verified in L4-REP-001
answered_questions.how_to_update_golden_file: Update ExpectedFragility in each builder function in internal/integration/replay.go

name: Version regression: all scenarios within 0.01% of golden
aim: Current build must produce topology sensitivity within 0.0100% of stored golden for all 6 scenarios

threshold.rationale: Any change in sensitivity algorithm must be intentional and explicitly reviewed — 0.01% catches real changes, not float noise

on_exceed: Topology sensitivity algorithm changed between this commit and the golden-writing commit — review internal/modelling/topology_sensitivity.go diff before proceeding

answered_questions.what_was_tested: ComputeTopologySensitivity output for 6 scenarios compared against golden files written by L4-REP-002
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 0.01% is tighter than the write tolerance (0.001%) to catch regression; still above float64 noise floor
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: An algorithm change altered risk scores — operators may see wrong fragility or perturbation rankings
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: Same inputs as L4-REP-002; golden files are the invariant anchor
answered_questions.is_golden_file_frozen: Yes — update ONLY when algorithm change is intentional and reviewed
answered_questions.how_to_update_golden_file: Delete golden files and re-run L4-REP-002, then confirm all scenarios produce expected outputs

name: Triangle recovery convergence within 1% of baseline
aim: After 3 recovery steps, fragility must be within 1.0% of healthy baseline

threshold.rationale: Step 3 edges are nearly identical to healthy baseline — convergence within 1% validates the recovery model

on_exceed: Recovery scenario step 3 does not return to baseline within 1% — either BuildTriangleRecoveryScenario(3) edges need updating or sensitivity is over-penalising residual degradation

answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard shows incomplete recovery even after all edges are restored — operators confused about system health
answered_questions.how_determinism_was_verified: All topology inputs are fixed (no time/random) — computation is deterministic
